read_when: deciding whether to continue or kill a workstream
primary_project_thesis: Build a GitHub-backed, file-tree-orchestrated AI operations system that remembers itself, treats clients more personally, and lets agents safely continue work across time.
core_distinction: This is not RAG as memory. This is FileTree orchestration: living directories, local ledgers, source-of-truth files, audit trails, visual maps, and GitHub-backed continuity.
core_operating_principle: Progress should be judged against purpose, not just activity.

goals.name: Durable Project Memory
goals.statement: The project should remember itself through GitHub-backed files, ledgers, maps, and local directory context.
goals.why_it_matters: Agents should not restart from zero. Humans should not have to repeatedly explain architecture, decisions, preferences, risks, and current state.
violates: Relying on chat history as primary memory
Leaving major decisions undocumented
Making important local-only changes without pushing
Changing systems without updating continuity files
Letting generated output become confused with truth
progress_signals: Ledgers stay current without becoming noise

goals.name: FileTree Orchestration Over RAG Memory
goals.statement: The project should use the versioned file tree as the primary memory and orchestration layer.
goals.why_it_matters: Jake wants durable, inspectable, localizable, GitHub-backed continuity — not vague retrieval over scattered memory.
supports: Directory-specific Boxes (Auto/Client Boxes/, Auto/Staff Boxes/, Auto/orchestrator/, Auto/comeketo-inbox/)
Visual maps generated from repo truth
violates: Hidden memory that cannot be audited
Agents guessing from vague context
Unstructured notes disconnected from the repo
Global memory replacing local directory truth
progress_signals: Agents can identify what area they are in
Local rules travel with the folder

goals.name: Box-Based Local Intelligence
goals.statement: Every important project area should become a Box: a living directory with memory, rules, source-of-truth notes, local ledgers, and handoff context.
goals.why_it_matters: Complexity should stay local when local. One client's constraints should not force a rewrite of the whole automation system.
violates: Solving every local issue globally
Creating one giant automation brain
Spreading one client's risk across every client
Making agents infer directory rules from filenames alone
progress_signals: Agents can enter a directory and know how to behave
Folder-specific risks are visible in place

goals.name: Automation That Feels More Human, Not Less
goals.statement: Automation should help Comeketo treat people with more memory, care, specificity, and respect — not blast generic manipulative drips.
goals.why_it_matters: The goal is meaningful client handling, not robotic follow-up. Good automation remembers what people said, avoids generic nonsense, respects timing, and helps André show up prepared.
violates: Creepy personalization
Surfacing enrichment-only facts to clients
Generic NEPQ trickery
Sending stale messages after replies
Forcing the same plan across every client
Over-contacting to show activity
progress_signals: Clients are not surprised by creepy researched facts
Automation creates useful movement without sacrificing trust

goals.name: Safe Movement Before Aggressive Autonomy
goals.statement: Especially during early proof periods, the system should create visible movement while keeping risky autonomy constrained.
goals.why_it_matters: Comeketo wants to see automation working, but early trust is fragile. The system should prove reliability before making bold sales plays automatically. The Brenda & Steve fee-waiver lesson is operative.
violates: Silent fee waivers
Hidden discounts in batch approval
Unapproved pricing claims
Enrichment-based customer-facing claims
Over-messaging to look busy
Auto-sending big rescue plays without isolated approval
progress_signals: Automation moves leads without creating panic
Early clients get safer, cleaner follow-up
goals.operative_case_study: Brenda & Steve fee-waiver audit (2026-04-28): system suggested bold move; risk was insufficiently isolated in batch approval; resolution = isolated approval cards required for fee waivers, discounts, scope promises, pricing claims, enrichment-based personalization.

goals.name: Source-of-Truth Discipline
goals.statement: Before changing information, agents should know where the truth lives.
goals.why_it_matters: The project has generated views, UI pages, local files, GitHub state, Close data, comms logs, transcripts, profiles, plans, and ledgers. Confusing a view for truth creates dangerous edits.
supports: Client Boxes as canonical lead substrate
violates: Editing generated output instead of source files
Trusting UI display over canonical box data
Treating profiles as customer-facing truth
Using stale plan text over current comms/state
Ignoring Done Gate updates
progress_signals: Agents know which file to edit
Generated state is not hand-edited accidentally
Client plans defer to comms and guardrails

goals.name: Rebuildability and Reconstruction
goals.statement: Important systems should be rebuildable from prompts, recipes, ledgers, source files, and documented intent.
goals.why_it_matters: The project should survive model changes, refactors, agent handoffs, local machine issues, and future upgrades.
violates: One-off magic
Undocumented generation
Unclear why a file exists
Inability to recreate a page, box, widget, or workflow
Hiding important context only in chat
progress_signals: Future agents can rebuild major pieces

goals.name: Visual Comprehension Beside Text Memory
goals.statement: The project should pair durable markdown/JSON memory with visual maps where shape matters.
goals.why_it_matters: Agents need structure. Humans need shape. Complex systems become easier to operate when file trees, dependency graphs, page maps, state flows, and timelines are visible.
supports: Dashboard views generated from ledger files
violates: Walls of text for inherently visual relationships
Disconnected diagrams that do not map to repo truth
Pretty visuals with no operational use
Visualizations that become stale and unlinked
progress_signals: Humans can understand the project faster
UI/data flows are easier to audit
Maps are generated or updated with ledger changes

goals.name: Agent Handoff Continuity
goals.statement: Any capable agent should be able to enter the repo, read the right files, and continue the work safely.
goals.why_it_matters: The project should not depend on one chat thread, one model, or one human's memory.
supports: Clear read-first order: Global → Temporal → relevant Box → work
violates: Ambiguous handoffs
Undocumented partial work
Unclear next steps
No record of what was attempted
Future agents needing to rediscover context
progress_signals: Stale work is easier to identify

goals.name: Defense as a First-Class Build Activity
goals.statement: The project should treat cleanup, ledgers, maps, audits, source-of-truth updates, and definitions of done as core build work.
goals.why_it_matters: A build that remembers itself compounds. A build that does not remember itself rots.
violates: Shipping code with no continuity updates
Ignoring maps and ledgers
Treating documentation as optional decoration
Building features faster than the system can remember them

guiding_principles.name: The repo is memory
guiding_principles.statement: The repo is not just code. It is the memory of the build.

guiding_principles.name: The box is truth
guiding_principles.statement: For local systems, trust the Box before global assumptions.

guiding_principles.name: Plans are hypotheses
guiding_principles.statement: Plans guide action but do not override comms, state, guardrails, or approvals.

guiding_principles.name: Movement should earn trust
guiding_principles.statement: Automation should create progress without making unsafe assumptions.

guiding_principles.name: Wholesome enrichment
guiding_principles.statement: Use context to be more respectful and relevant, not to perform creepy intimacy.

guiding_principles.name: Source before surface
guiding_principles.statement: Before changing visible behavior, identify the source of truth behind the surface.

guiding_principles.name: If it changed, memory should change
guiding_principles.statement: Meaningful work should update the relevant continuity layer.

guiding_principles.name: Local complexity stays local
guiding_principles.statement: Do not rewrite the whole machine for one client's edge case if a Box-level rule solves it.

guiding_principles.name: Visuals are comprehension tools
guiding_principles.statement: Use visual maps where relationships matter.

guiding_principles.name: Agents need runway
guiding_principles.statement: Leave enough context for the next agent to continue safely.

anti_goals: a pile of disconnected local files
a UI that hides where truth lives
an automation system that makes financial commitments silently
a system that uses enrichment creepily
a codebase where only the original builder understands the structure
a dashboard that looks useful but cannot be trusted
a project that moves fast by deleting its own memory

tradeoff_rules.rule: Prefer slightly slower work that preserves memory over fast work that future agents cannot continue.
Prefer local Box fixes when the problem is local. Use global rules only when the pattern repeats or creates systemic risk.
Prefer safe movement over aggressive autonomy during early proof. Escalate risky moves to approval.
Use enrichment to improve internal understanding. Do not surface it unless grounded in comms or approved.
A beautiful UI that obscures truth is worse than a plain UI that clearly reflects source state.
If changing behavior changes ownership, data binding, or expectations, update the relevant ledger or sitemap.
Treat cleanup, ledger updates, audit markers, and source-of-truth updates as build work, not chores. NS-10 makes this a first-class activity.

audit_questions: Which North Star goal does this support?
Does this change preserve or damage continuity?
Does it make truth easier or harder to locate?
Does it create local memory where local memory is needed?
Does it make automation more human or more robotic?
Does it require an approval gate?
Does it update the right ledgers?
Would a future agent understand why this exists?
Can this be rebuilt or audited later?
client_box_audit_questions: Does the plan reflect current comms and state?
Are risky moves explicitly approved (isolated, not batched)?
Does a reply make this plan stale?
Is the next move safe enough to surface/send?

wholesome_enrichment.principle: Enrichment is used to understand context, not to perform surveillance.
allowed_internal: Prepare André for a better conversation
not_allowed_without_approval: Assumptions about identity, family, wealth, religion, politics, health, or personal life
Manipulative pressure based on inferred vulnerability

client_automation_north_star.core: Comeketo automation should make client handling more remembered, more prepared, and more respectful. The system should not merely increase touch count.
good_automated_move: Reflect what the client actually said
Ask for a real next step
Preserve context for the next interaction

current_alignment_notes.operative_tension: The system should be smart enough to suggest bold moves, but not autonomous enough to hide risky commitments inside batch approval. Resolution: bold moves welcome in plans; risky bold moves require isolated approval cards.

definition_of_done_rule: A meaningful task is not fully done until it has either: supported a North Star goal, preserved a North Star goal, removed a threat to a North Star goal, or explicitly recorded why it was necessary despite not mapping cleanly to one.
final_operating_rule: Do not confuse movement with progress. Progress means the project became more durable, more truthful, more humane, more understandable, more rebuildable, or safer to continue. If work supports a North Star, notice it. If work threatens a North Star, surface it. If work serves no North Star, question it.
